target.name: Watershed
target.founder: Taylor Francis, Christian Anderson, Avi Itskovich

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $1.8B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Watershed is a Series A–stage climate and energy company founded by Taylor Francis, Christian Anderson, and Avi Itskovich that has raised over $200M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of nine private companies and one public comparable (LNZA) in the climate and energy sector, including same-stage peers Graphyte ($200M), Fourth Power ($150M), and Steady Energy ($110M).

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, anchored to Watershed's last post-money valuation of $1.8B, applies a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A advancement, yielding $3.6B. Peer-median analysis across three same-sector, same-stage comparables produces $150M, with a range spanning $110M to $200M. The material divergence between these methods reflects Watershed's substantially higher current valuation relative to the observed peer cohort.

Triangulation across both methods implies a valuation range of $128M (low) to $4.1B (high), with a median of $1.9B. The $1.9B median is consistent with Watershed's stated $1.8B current valuation. Key uncertainties include the representativeness of the peer set—which skews smaller—and the applicability of stage-progression assumptions to a company that may command significant capital efficiency or technology premium relative to peers. A tighter range requires clarity on Watershed's revenue scale, customer concentration, and unit economics relative to comparables.
